label: CNS kinase, prioritize BBB
context: Lead optimization for a brain-penetrant DYRK1A kinase inhibitor. Target tissue is CNS. We need to PRIORITIZE blood-brain-barrier permeability (BBB_Martins) and keep TPSA below 90. Tolerable risks are mild CYP2D6 inhibition. Hard stops: hERG > 0.5 (cardiotoxicity), Skin Reaction > 0.6, MW > 500, LogP > 5.

label: Oral gram-positive antibacterial
context: Oral systemic gram-positive antibacterial lead optimization. Target is bacterial cell wall. We need oral bioavailability and good metabolic stability. PRIORITIZE Bioavailability_Ma, HIA_Hou, Half_Life_Obach. BBB penetration is irrelevant (we want low-CNS). Hard stops: hERG > 0.5, DILI > 0.6, AMES > 0.5.

label: Topical anti-inflammatory
context: Topical anti-inflammatory lead optimization. Application is dermal. We need LOW skin reactivity and acceptable lipophilicity for skin penetration. PRIORITIZE LogP in [2,4], minimize Skin_Reaction. Systemic ADMET (CYP, hERG) matters less because the drug is local. Hard stops: Skin_Reaction > 0.4, Carcinogens_Lagunin > 0.5.

label: Oncology kinase, accept moderate CYP
context: Oncology lead optimization for a tumor-cell-targeted kinase inhibitor. Oral systemic delivery. We tolerate moderate CYP risk because oncology patients accept higher liabilities. PRIORITIZE QED, Bioavailability_Ma, and avoid AMES. BBB is neutral. Hard stops: hERG > 0.6 (still cardiotoxicity matters), AMES > 0.5.
